obligations.reason: The scope exposes JTAG pins and debug signals including jtag_hash, jtag_unlock, dmi_req.op, debug_req_o, ndmreset_o, and DM slave/master paths. Check whether the visible RTL gates all security-relevant debug operations, not merely whether debug logic exists.
obligations.subject: external JTAG/DTM debug requester
obligations.object: debug module access to hart control, memory/register access signals, ndmreset_o, dmactive_o, debug_req_o
obligations.expected_guard: debug/JTAG operations that can issue debug requests, reset, or access memory/register state should be mediated by an explicit authorization condition such as jtag_unlock, authenticated DMI state, debug_mode_i policy, or lifecycle/test-mode restriction before reaching protected objects.
obligations.candidate_path: JTAG/DTM inputs tck_i/tms_i/td_i/trst_ni -> i_dmi_jtag/SimJTAG/DTM debug request signals -> debug module/slave/master paths
required_evidence_slots: observed RTL gating or missing gating
path/reachability through instantiated debug blocks and assignments
obligations.uncertainty: Fact layer lists signals and instances but does not show all debug module connections or preprocessor variants; inspect full riscv_peripherals.sv around debug instantiation and signal assignments.

obligations.reason: Visible assignment gates rom_req with acc_ctrl_c[priv_lvl_i][0], while bootrom read data and boot selection are present. Verify the guard covers the actual response path and all request variants.
obligations.subject: NoC/AXI peripheral requester with priv_lvl_i
obligations.operation: read boot ROM via NoC/AXI peripheral path
obligations.expected_guard: boot ROM reads should be allowed only for authorized privilege/access-control entries; address decode and read enable should be gated by acc_ctrl_c[priv_lvl_i][bootrom_index] or equivalent and should not have an alternate ungated read response path.
obligations.candidate_path: NoC/AXI peripheral request -> rom_req_acct/rom_addr -> rom_req -> bootrom instances -> rom_rdata/NoC response
state_conditions_to_check: priv_lvl_i values used as acc_ctrl_c index
whether rom_rdata is exposed when rom_req is false
required_evidence_slots: observed request and data mux behavior
obligations.uncertainty: Only selected assignments are visible; inspect request/response mux and bootrom instantiations to confirm no data leakage independent of rom_req.

obligations.reason: The top-level contains reglk_ctrl and acc_ctrl arrays and external reglk/acct NoC ports. These are security policy assets; verify their write paths are privileged and sticky/locked as intended.
obligations.subject: software-visible NoC/AXI requester
obligations.operation: configure or overwrite peripheral access policy/lock bits
obligations.object: reglk_ctrl and acc_ctrl access-control policy state
obligations.expected_guard: writes to access-control and register-lock configuration registers should be restricted by privilege/accounting policy and protected by lock bits; once locked, lock/configuration state should not be cleared or bypassed except by reset or authorized lifecycle transition.
obligations.candidate_path: NoC/AXI register access -> reglk_ctrl/acc_ctrl programming -> acc_ctrl_c privilege matrix used by peripheral request gates
state_conditions_to_check: reset defaults of reglk_ctrl and acc_ctrl
whether reglk_ctrl bits can be cleared after set
whether acc_ctrl can be modified while locked
whether debug/test paths can write these registers
required_evidence_slots: subject: software/NoC requester writing acct/reglk registers
operation: write/configure lock or access policy
expected guard: privilege check plus existing lock bit
observed write enables and data path
obligations.uncertainty: Fact layer shows signals and ports but not all always blocks; inspect register write decode and reset logic around acct/reglk handling.

obligations.reason: DMA can act as an independent memory requester. Visible wrapper inputs include reglk_ctrl_i, acct_ctrl_i, we_flag and configuration registers; verify every write/start path is mediated.
obligations.subject: AXI/NoC requester configuring DMA
obligations.operation: write DMA configuration and trigger transfer
obligations.object: DMA start, length, source_addr, dest_addr, core_lock configuration registers
obligations.expected_guard: DMA configuration and start should be allowed only when acct_ctrl_i grants the requester and relevant reglk_ctrl_i/core_lock permits writes; start must not bypass locked configuration or privilege policy.
obligations.candidate_path: NoC/AXI request -> DMA wrapper registers start/length/source/dest/core_lock -> dma engine -> valid_o/done and memory transfer controls
state_conditions_to_check: reset value of core_lock_reg and config registers
whether done/end state allows unintended restart
required_evidence_slots: object: DMA control/address registers and transfer trigger
obligations.uncertainty: Need full wrapper decode around line 129 and dma instantiation to determine whether lock/accounting inputs are consistently used.

obligations.reason: DMA module has PMP-related registers and an allow register plus source/destination address registers. Check that authorization is address-specific, access-type-specific, and updated for active transfers.
obligations.subject: DMA engine
obligations.operation: DMA read/write transfer to configured memory addresses
obligations.object: memory regions reachable by DMA source/destination addresses
obligations.expected_guard: each DMA source and destination access should be checked against PMP/range permissions for the correct access type before issuing/validating the transfer; stale pmp_allow_reg must not authorize new addresses or changed transfer parameters.
obligations.candidate_path: DMA engine pmp_addr_reg/pmp_access_type/pmp_allow_reg -> pmp module/pmp_entry -> valid_reg/valid_o transfer authorization
state_conditions_to_check: pmp_allow_reg update conditions versus address updates
required_evidence_slots: subject: DMA engine as bus master
expected guard: PMP allow for current address and operation
obligations.uncertainty: Fact layer does not include pmp_access_type signal name details or state machine; inspect full dma.sv sequencing.

obligations.reason: PMP modules are in scope with allow_o/match_o and range calculation signals. Verify no address-mode, off-by-one, priority, or default-allow behavior grants unauthorized accesses.
obligations.subject: memory access requester using PMP check
obligations.operation: authorize memory access based on PMP entry match
obligations.expected_guard: PMP matching should enforce configured address mode/range, privilege, and access type consistently; default no-match behavior should deny protected accesses unless explicitly permitted by architecture/policy.
obligations.candidate_path: pmp configuration inputs conf_addr_i/conf_addr_prev_i/riscv cfg -> pmp_entry match_o -> pmp allow_o
required_evidence_slots: subject: core or DMA memory access requester
expected guard: PMP entry match plus access type/privilege bits
observed match priority and allow logic
obligations.uncertainty: Port extraction labels several riscv typed ports generically; inspect actual pmpcfg, access type, and privilege ports.

obligations.reason: CLINT interface exposes AXI-lite write enables and top-level CLINT NoC ports, with timer_irq_o and ipi_o outputs. Check whether privileged-only interrupt control is enforced in visible RTL.
obligations.subject: AXI/NoC requester to CLINT
obligations.operation: write CLINT MMIO registers controlling interrupts/timer
obligations.object: CLINT timer compare/software interrupt state driving timer_irq_o and ipi_o
obligations.expected_guard: writes to CLINT timer/interrupt registers should be mediated by address decode and privilege/access policy at the wrapper/top-level; unauthorized requesters should not be able to raise IPIs or alter timer interrupts.
obligations.candidate_path: CLINT AXI-lite ariane_axi -> address_o/en_o/we_o/data_o -> timer/software interrupt registers
state_conditions_to_check: address decode for CLINT register offsets
read/write response when access is denied
obligations.uncertainty: Only the AXI-lite bridge ports are visible; inspect top-level CLINT integration and register module if present.

obligations.reason: Top-level includes many security-sensitive NoC peripheral ports and fuse signals. Verify per-peripheral request gates and response muxes use acc_ctrl/reglk where appropriate and do not leak secret data through stale or ungated responses.
obligations.subject: NoC requester to security peripherals
obligations.operation: read/write security peripheral registers or expose secret/fuse data
obligations.object: crypto accelerator state, RNG data, HMAC/RSA/AES controls, fuse_rdata/fuse_addr
obligations.expected_guard: reads/writes to crypto, RNG, HMAC, RSA, and fuse interfaces should be gated by per-peripheral access-control and lock policy, and secret/fuse/key data should not be exposed on NoC responses unless explicitly authorized.
obligations.candidate_path: NoC ports for crypto/security peripherals (aes0/1/2, sha256, hmac, rsa, rng, fuse) -> request/response muxes -> rdata outputs
state_conditions_to_check: fuse_rdata validity when fuse_req is false
acc_ctrl_c index mapping for each peripheral
required_evidence_slots: subject: NoC requester with privilege level
operation: read/write peripheral or fuse state
expected guard: acc_ctrl_c plus reglk/lock and address decode
obligations.uncertainty: Fact layer is truncated for many assignments; inspect full riscv_peripherals.sv request generation and response mux sections.

obligations.reason: The top-level exposes a reset NoC interface and per-peripheral reset signals, and DMA wrapper has rst_8 input. Check whether reset writes can bypass locks or clear protected state.
obligations.subject: NoC/software reset requester
obligations.operation: trigger peripheral reset or clear security state
obligations.object: peripheral reset signals rst_1..rst_13 and security/lock state affected by reset
obligations.expected_guard: software-visible or NoC-controlled resets of protected peripherals should require privilege/access-control authorization and should not clear lock/security policy state unless allowed by lifecycle/reset policy.
obligations.candidate_path: reset-control NoC port buf_ariane_rst_* -> rst_1..rst_13/rst_8 -> peripheral reset inputs and DMA rst_8
state_conditions_to_check: reset register write decode and acc_ctrl gate
which state elements are cleared by rst_N versus global rst_ni
whether reglk_ctrl/acc_ctrl are reset by software reset
required_evidence_slots: subject: NoC requester to reset peripheral
object: peripheral reset lines and lock/config state
observed write and reset generation logic
obligations.uncertainty: Need full reset register decode in riscv_peripherals and reset use sites in wrappers.

obligations.reason: The scope has testmode_i, simulation debug instances, JTAG assignments, and protected policy/security assets. Explicitly check for bypass paths rather than assuming debug presence is vulnerable.
obligations.subject: debug/JTAG/test requester
obligations.operation: read/write/override protected state through debug or test path
obligations.object: protected peripheral state, DMA configuration, fuse/crypto data, access-control registers
obligations.expected_guard: testmode_i, SimDTM/SimJTAG, and debug paths must not bypass normal acc_ctrl/reglk/PMP checks for protected registers or secrets unless explicitly confined to simulation/lifecycle-authorized mode.
state_conditions_to_check: whether debug writes use same register write enables as NoC
required_evidence_slots: expected guard: auth/lifecycle plus normal lock/access controls
obligations.uncertainty: Requires tracing debug/test signals through preprocessor branches and instantiated modules not fully included in fact layer.
